title: quercus spinosa subsp. miyabei
intro: Quercus spinosa subsp. miyabei, also known as Miyabe's scrub oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to East Asia, characterized by its broad leaves and distinctive branching patterns. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and digestive problems. Quercus spinosa subsp. miyabei is believed to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Quercus spinosa subsp. miyabei include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
